subject: Product Management
title: PMF Is Never Done
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on PMF Is Never Done.

hook.text: The most dangerous moment for a product is when everyone celebrates PMF and stops looking for threats.

buildup.text: Markets evolve. Customer expectations rise. Competitors improve. The fit that works today may not work next year.

discovery.text: Amazon's Jeff Bezos calls it 'Day 1' thinking — always acting like you're still searching for fit, even at massive scale.

twist.text: Day 2 is stasis. Then irrelevance. Then decline. Then death. Bezos says Day 2 happens when you stop obsessing over customers.

climax.text: The best product companies treat PMF as an ongoing practice — continuously measuring, learning, and adapting.

punchline.text: PMF is never done. Stay on Day 1. Always.

quiz.question: Why is PMF never truly 'done'?
options: Markets evolve, competitors improve, and customer expectations rise continuously
PMF can only be achieved once
Once PMF is found, the product never needs updating
